events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: no expansion tank on a closed system for a water heater. This is a code compliance issue - IPC 504 and IRC P2804 require expansion tanks on closed systems. This creates a hazard of excessive pressure buildup, potential tank rupture, T&P valve discharge, and potential water damage.

Let me construct the JSON response.

- Decision: fail (non-compliant condition)
- Risk: medium (pressure buildup hazard, potential water damage, T&P valve cycling)
- Work stage: in-progress
- Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
- Percent complete: estimate based on in-progress work, maybe 60
- Findings: no expansion tank on closed system
- Actions: install expansion tank, verify T&P valve
- References: IPC 504, IRC P2804

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 60,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "No expansion tank installed on a closed water heater system, allowing thermal expansion pressure to build with no relief path other than the T&P valve",
    "Non-compliant with IPC 504 / IRC P2804 requirements for thermal expansion control on closed systems",
    "Risk of repeated T&P valve weeping/discharge, premature valve failure, and potential tank or piping damage from cyclic overpressure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a properly sized thermal expansion tank on the cold water supply line upstream of the water heater and downstream of any backflow preventer or pressure regulator",
    "Verify system pressure and confirm the T&P valve is correctly sized, installed, and not weeping",
    "Confirm the expansion tank pre-charge pressure matches the system static pressure",
    "Re-inspect for compliance after correction"
  ],
  "rationale": "A closed system with a pressure-reducing valve or backflow preventer has no path for thermal expansion, so code requires an expansion tank. Its absence is a non-compliant installed defect that creates cyclic overpressure, risking T&P valve discharge and component damage.",
  "references": ["IPC 504", "IRC P2804"]
}
